title: gymnogramma fendleri
intro: Gymnogramma fendleri is a type of fern, specifically a species of the genus Gymnogramma, a group of vascular plants that belong to the family Pteridaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and is known for its medicinal properties. Gymnogramma fendleri has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant's leaves can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, or salves, which have been used to treat a range of health problems, including wounds, burns, and respiratory issues.
